authoritySource: OET Platform — Remediation Rulebook (V0 stub for the AI-personalisation feature flag).
stubExplanation: This file is a placeholder so the rulebook directory exists alongside writing/, speaking/, grammar/, etc. The RemediationPlanService.EnableAiPersonalisation flag is gated false until: (1) RuleKind.Remediation is added to the .NET enum + folder switch, (2) the AiGatewayService prompt builder gets a Remediation branch, (3) this file is replaced with real grounded content (see docs/MOCKS-RANDOMISATION.md and docs/audits/rulebook-compliance-2026-05-11-closure.md for the schedule).

rules.title: Address the candidate directly
rules.body: All AI-generated remediation plan summaries must address the candidate in the second person ('you'). Never refer to the candidate by name, never use third person.

rules.title: No clinical advice
rules.body: Remediation summaries are about exam preparation, not clinical care. Never offer medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment recommendations even if the underlying mock content is clinical.

rules.title: No invented rule IDs
rules.body: Summaries must not invent or fabricate rule identifiers. The deterministic plan and weakness tags shipped by RemediationPlanService are the only ground truth.

rules.title: Never produce a candidate score
rules.body: Remediation summaries must never project, estimate, or infer an OET scaled score, grade letter, or pass/fail outcome. Scoring is the exclusive responsibility of OetScoring.

rules.title: Frame the week, not the day list
rules.body: Summaries must frame the focus of the week (e.g. 'this week prioritises Listening Part B and Reading Part C') rather than reciting the day-by-day list verbatim — the deterministic UI already shows the day list.

rules.title: Stay under 240 characters
rules.body: Summaries are advisory, not instructional, and must stay under 240 characters total so the layout remains compact.

rules.title: Encourage without false confidence
rules.body: Use encouraging tone but avoid promising a band-7 outcome or any outcome the deterministic data cannot support.

criticalRuleSummary: Frame the week, not the day list.
